title: Concurrency Budgets, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Worker Threads · Inference Brief: Admission Concurrency Queue
summary: The service launches unbounded promises, blocks the event loop with CPU work, uses one global rate counter, buffers queues without rejection, and cannot explain overload recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts admission concurrency queue increment in admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound active requests, per-dependency concurrency, queue length, queue age, memory, rejection, Retry-After, and fairness separately.
Distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure CPU blocking, synchronous I/O, long callbacks, and event-loop delay.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Admission Concurrency Queue · Theory Admission Concurrency Queue
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2773 in a administrative API crossing two identity and network trust zones. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine concurrency, queue, tenant-rate, and bounded CPU-offload decisions; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound active requests, per-dependency concurrency, queue length, queue age, memory, rejection, Retry-After, and fairness separately.
